name: Short-term GlyNAC dose trial clarified redox biomarkers but did not test functional aging benefit
summary: A larger two-week GlyNAC trial supports redox-biomarker context and dosing, but remains short and biomarker-focused.
statement: In generally healthy older adults, a two-week dose-ranging GlyNAC trial found elevated baseline oxidative-stress markers and lower reduced-to-oxidized glutathione ratio versus young adults, and tested whether GlyNAC could shift glutathione/redox biomarkers and amino-acid availability.
population_or_model: Generally healthy older adults aged 60 to 85 years, with a young-adult reference group
quantitative_note: One hundred seventeen older adults were randomized and 114 completed treatment; 20 young adults served as a non-interventional reference group.
caveats: The trial was only two weeks and did not test functional aging endpoints.
It was designed around glutathione and oxidative-damage biomarkers, not durable mitochondrial or healthspan outcomes.
The source reports Nestle Health Science involvement and patents or licenses related to glycine and NAC use.
